Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Webhooks look simple: something happens in your system, then you send an HTTP POST to another system.
In production, that POST is where reliability problems start. The customer endpoint can be down. DNS can fail. TLS can break. Their API can return 500. Your process can restart after sending the webhook but before recording success.
A reliable webhook system needs durable delivery state, retries with backoff, signatures, idempotency, and visibility into failed deliveries.
In this article, we will build a production-style webhook delivery system in ASP.NET Core using EF Core, background workers, HMAC signatures, retry metadata, and safe delivery claiming.
The Architecture
The application does not send webhooks directly inside the user request. Instead, it records webhook deliveries in the database. A background worker claims pending deliveries and sends them later.
This gives us durability, retry behavior, and operational visibility.
The Webhook Event Type
Use explicit event names. They become part of your public integration contract.
